reportText:
# The State of Cybersecurity 2025-2026

## Executive Summary

The global cybersecurity landscape continues to deteriorate at an alarming pace. The average cost of a data breach reached $4.88 million in 2024 — the highest level ever recorded. Ransomware attacks now occur every 11 seconds, with average ransom demands exceeding $2.1 million. Despite $215 billion in global cybersecurity spending, the workforce gap has widened to 4.8 million unfilled positions.

## Threat Landscape

Phishing remains the dominant attack vector, accounting for 41% of all breaches. The rise of generative AI has supercharged social engineering — attackers can now craft highly convincing, personalized phishing campaigns at scale. Supply chain attacks grew 200% year-over-year, with the software supply chain emerging as the single largest unmanaged risk surface.

## Economic Impact

The economic toll extends beyond direct breach costs. Stock prices decline an average of 7.5% following a major breach disclosure, and 60% of small businesses that suffer a cyberattack close within six months. Healthcare remains the most targeted industry, with average breach costs of $10.93 million.

## The Path Forward

Zero Trust Architecture adoption has reached 61%, and organizations with mature Zero Trust programs save $1.76 million per breach on average. AI-powered threat detection reduces breach lifecycles by 108 days — the difference between a contained incident and a catastrophic one. The single highest-ROI investment remains employee security training, which reduces successful phishing attacks by 70%.
